member.name: T:SecureRequest.Crypto.IRsaPublicKeyProvider
member.summary: Provides the server's RSA public key so it can be exposed to clients. Implement or resolve this to serve the key from a controller / minimal-API endpoint.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.IRsaPublicKeyProvider.GetPublicKeyBase64
member.summary: Returns the RSA public key encoded as a Base64 SPKI (SubjectPublicKeyInfo) string.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService
member.summary: Cryptographic operations used by . The default implementation uses RSA-OAEP-SHA256, AES-256-GCM, and HMAC-SHA256.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService.DecryptSecretKey(System.Byte[])
member.summary: RSA-OAEP decrypts the per-request secret key (64 bytes: 32 AES + 32 HMAC).

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService.Decrypt(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: AES-256-GCM decrypts using .

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService.ComputeBodyHash(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Returns the SHA-256 hex digest of (empty-body constant when input is empty).

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService.ComputeSignature(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary:
Builds and HMAC-SHA256 signs the canonical string: METHOD
PATH
QUERY
TIMESTAMP
NONCE
BODY_HEX_HASH

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.ISecureRequestCryptoService.ValidateSignature(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Constant-time comparison of two Base64 signature strings.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider
member.summary:
Singleton that holds the server's RSA-2048 key pair in memory.
Lifecycle: - On first startup checks the distributed cache. - If a persisted PKCS-8 private key is found it calls . - Otherwise it calls and stores the result. - All server instances behind a load balancer therefore share the same key pair.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider.LoadFromPrivateKey(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Loads an existing PKCS-8 private key (retrieved from the distributed cache).

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider.GenerateAndExportPrivateKey
member.summary: Generates a fresh RSA-2048 key pair, stores it, and returns the PKCS-8 private key bytes so the caller can persist them to the distributed cache.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider.GetPublicKeySpki
member.summary: Exports the public key as a DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo (SPKI) byte array.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider.Decrypt(System.Byte[])
member.summary: RSA-OAEP-SHA256 decrypts using the private key.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Crypto.SecureRequestCryptoService
member.summary: Default implementation of . Algorithms: RSA-OAEP-SHA256 · AES-256-GCM · HMAC-SHA256 · SHA-256.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Crypto.SecureRequestCryptoService.#ctor(SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider)
member.summary: Initializes the service with the singleton .

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestServiceExtensions

M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestServiceExtensions.AddSecureRequest(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.Action{SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions})
member.summary: Registers all SecureRequest services and returns a for optional fluent configuration (e.g. custom key storage).
member.remarks: Unless .WithKeyStorage<T>() is called, the default key storage is which requires IDistributedCache to be registered (e.g. AddStackExchangeRedisCache(…) or AddDistributedMemoryCache()).

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestServiceExtensions.UseSecureRequest(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.IApplicationBuilder)
member.summary: Adds to the ASP.NET Core pipeline. Call this before UseRouting() / UseAuthorization().

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestServiceExtensions.MapSecureRequestPublicKey(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a minimal-API endpoint that returns the server's RSA public key as Base64. Clients call this once to cache the key for encrypting X-Encrypted-Key headers. Default route: GET /api/secure/public-key

Maps a minimal-API endpoint that returns the server's RSA public key as Base64. Clients call this once to cache the key for encrypting X-Encrypted-Key headers. Default route: GET /api/secure/public-key Responds with Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400 so clients cache it for 24 hours.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestServiceExtensions.MapSecureRequestKeyRotation(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.IEndpointRouteBuilder,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Maps a protected minimal-API endpoint that triggers RSA key rotation. Default route: POST /api/secure/rotate-key
⚠️ This endpoint MUST be protected — apply an authorization policy:
app.MapSecureRequestKeyRotation(policy: "AdminOnly");
After rotation, clients holding the old public key will receive a 422 on their next secured request, automatically re-fetch the new key, and retry transparently.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestBuilder
member.summary: Fluent builder returned by for chaining optional configuration such as custom key storage providers.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestBuilder.WithKeyStorage``1
member.summary:
Replaces the default with a custom implementation — e.g. Azure Key Vault, AWS KMS, Google Cloud Secret Manager, or HashiCorp Vault.
builder.Services .AddSecureRequest(builder.Configuration) .WithKeyStorage<AzureKeyVaultStorageProvider>();

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Extensions.SecureRequestBuilder.WithKeyStorage(System.Func{System.IServiceProvider,SecureRequest.KeyStorage.IRsaKeyStorageProvider})
member.summary:
Replaces the default key storage with a factory-based custom provider. Use this when your provider needs constructor arguments not available via DI.
builder.Services .AddSecureRequest(builder.Configuration) .WithKeyStorage(sp => new AwsSecretsManagerStorageProvider( sp.GetRequiredService<IAmazonSecretsManager>(), "my-secret-id"));

member.name: T:SecureRequest.KeyStorage.DistributedCacheKeyStorageProvider
member.summary:
Default implementation that persists the RSA private key in IDistributedCache (Redis, SQL, in-memory, etc.).
⚠️ SECURITY WARNING: This provider stores the RSA private key as plain Base64 in your distributed cache. Anyone with read access to Redis / your cache store can extract the private key and decrypt all X-Encrypted-Key headers, defeating the purpose of the encryption.
For production systems handling sensitive data, replace this with a dedicated Key Management Service using .WithKeyStorage<YourProvider>(): - Azure Key Vault → AzureKeyVaultStorageProvider - AWS KMS / Secrets Manager → AwsSecretsManagerStorageProvider - Google Cloud Secret Manager → GcpSecretManagerStorageProvider - HashiCorp Vault → custom IRsaKeyStorageProvider implementation
This default implementation is intentionally kept simple for development and low-sensitivity environments where the cache store is already trusted.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.KeyStorage.DistributedCacheKeyStorageProvider.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{SecureRequest.KeyStorage.DistributedCacheKeyStorageProvider})
member.summary: Initializes the provider with the distributed cache and logger.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.KeyStorage.IRsaKeyStorageProvider
M:SecureRequest.KeyStorage.IRsaKeyStorageProvider.LoadPrivateKeyAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads the RSA private key (PKCS-8 bytes) from the store. Returns null if no key has been stored yet — the caller will then generate a new one.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.KeyStorage.IRsaKeyStorageProvider.StorePrivateKeyAsync(System.Byte[],System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Persists the RSA private key (PKCS-8 bytes) to the store. Called once on first startup when no key exists yet.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Middleware.SecureRequestMiddleware
member.summary:
ASP.NET Core middleware that enforces the secure-request pipeline.
Steps (which run depends on and ):
1. Bypass check — OPTIONS, excluded paths, methods not in SecuredMethods → pass through. 2. Header presence — X-Timestamp + X-Nonce always; X-Encrypted-Key when crypto active; X-Signature when HMAC active. 3. RSA key decrypt — X-Encrypted-Key → 32-byte AES key + 32-byte HMAC key. 4. Timestamp check — rejects drift > TimestampToleranceSeconds. 5. Nonce check — rejects replayed nonces via IDistributedCache. 6. Body handling — decrypts when EnableBodyEncryption=true, reads raw otherwise. 7. HMAC verify — verifies X-Signature when EnableHmacSigning=true. 8. Nonce burn — stores nonce (TTL = NonceCacheTtlSeconds). 9. Body replace — swaps encrypted/raw body with plaintext for downstream handlers.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Middleware.SecureRequestMiddleware.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.RequestDelegate,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{SecureRequest.Middleware.SecureRequestMiddleware},Microsoft.Extensions.Options.IOptions{SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions})
member.summary: Initializes the middleware with the pipeline delegate, logger, and options.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Middleware.SecureRequestMiddleware.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache)
member.summary: Processes the HTTP request through the secure-request pipeline.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Middleware.SecureRequestMiddleware.TryBase64Decode(System.Byte[],System.Byte[]@)
member.summary: Tries multiple decoding strategies to handle different Content-Type side-effects: text/plain → raw base64 (correct) application/json → JSON-quoted: "BASE64==" application/x-www-form-urlencoded → URL-encoded: + → %2B, / → %2F

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions
member.summary:
Configuration for the RSA + AES-256-GCM + HMAC secure-request pipeline. Bind from appsettings under the key passed to AddSecureRequest() (default: "SecureRequest").
Feature matrix — EnableBodyEncryption and EnableHmacSigning are independent: true + true → full pipeline — body encrypted AND signature verified (recommended for production) false + true → integrity only — body sent as plaintext, HMAC signature still verified true + false → confidentiality only — body encrypted, signature check skipped false + false → anti-replay only — timestamp + nonce checked, no crypto on body Enabled=false → bypass everything (useful in development / Swagger)

member.name: F:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.DefaultSectionName
member.summary: Default appsettings section name: "SecureRequest".

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.Enabled
member.summary: Master switch. When false the entire middleware is bypassed. Default: false — opt-in per environment.

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.EnableBodyEncryption
member.summary: When true the client AES-256-GCM encrypts the request body and the server decrypts it before passing the plaintext to controllers. Requires X-Encrypted-Key header. Default: true.

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.EnableHmacSigning
member.summary: When true the client HMAC-SHA256 signs a canonical string over the request and the server verifies the X-Signature header. Requires X-Encrypted-Key header. Default: true.

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.TimestampToleranceSeconds
member.summary: Maximum allowed clock-skew between client and server in seconds. Default: 300 s (5 minutes).

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.NonceCacheTtlSeconds
member.summary: How long a consumed nonce is retained in the cache to block replays. Must be greater than 2 × . Default: 700 s.

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.SecuredMethods
member.summary: HTTP methods the pipeline enforces. Requests whose method is not in this list are bypassed automatically. Default: POST, PUT, PATCH.

member.name: P:SecureRequest.Options.SecureRequestOptions.ExcludedPaths
member.summary: URL path segments (case-insensitive) that bypass the pipeline. The match is a substring check on the full display URL. Example: "/api/public" bypasses anything whose URL contains that segment.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Services.ISecureRequestKeyRotationService
member.summary:
Provides RSA key pair rotation for the secure-request pipeline.
Key rotation flow: 1. Generates a new RSA-2048 key pair. 2. Persists the new private key via (overwrites the old key in Redis / Azure Key Vault / AWS KMS / etc.). 3. Loads the new key pair into memory — all future requests use the new keys immediately. 4. Clients that cached the old public key will receive a 422 on their next request, automatically clear their cache, re-fetch the new public key, and retry. (This is handled by the built-in 422 auto-retry in the frontend interceptor.)
When to rotate: - On a regular schedule (e.g. every 90 days via a cron job or scheduled task) - After a suspected key compromise - After deploying a new environment

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Services.ISecureRequestKeyRotationService.RotateKeyAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Rotates the RSA key pair: generates a new pair, persists it, and activates it immediately. Returns the new public key as Base64 so callers can log or audit it.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Services.RsaKeyInitializerService
member.summary:
Hosted service that initialises the RSA key pair exactly once at application startup.
Strategy: 1. Resolve — defaults to distributed cache, or a custom KMS provider registered via .WithKeyStorage<T>(). 2. Found → load it — all instances share the same key pair. 3. Not found → generate a new key pair, persist it via the storage provider.
This guarantees all server instances behind a load balancer always decrypt X-Encrypted-Key headers correctly regardless of which instance handles the request.

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Services.RsaKeyInitializerService.#ctor(SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider,Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceScopeFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{SecureRequest.Services.RsaKeyInitializerService})
member.summary: Initializes the service with the RSA key provider, scope factory, and logger.

member.name: T:SecureRequest.Services.SecureRequestKeyRotationService
member.summary: Default implementation of .

member.name: M:SecureRequest.Services.SecureRequestKeyRotationService.#ctor(SecureRequest.Crypto.RsaKeyProvider,SecureRequest.KeyStorage.IRsaKeyStorageProvider,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{SecureRequest.Services.SecureRequestKeyRotationService})
member.summary: Initializes the rotation service with the RSA key provider, storage provider, and logger.
